name: Book of Enoch Giants
description: The Book of Enoch, particularly 1 Enoch, describes the origins and nature of giants (Nephilim) who are said to have inhabited the Earth before the Great Flood. According to Enochian texts, these giants were the offspring of fallen angels (Watchers) who cohabitated with human women. The Book details how the Watchers taught humans forbidden knowledge, leading to corruption and ultimately God's decision to cleanse the Earth with the flood. Proponents of the literal interpretation of these texts often suggest that the skeletal remains of unusually large humans, archaeological anomalies, and ancient myths about giants provide evidence for the historical existence of these beings. Some connect the giants to lost civilizations or advanced technologies. Mainstream scholarly perspectives generally consider the Book of Enoch to be apocryphal, meaning it is not part of the canon recognized by most Jewish and Christian denominations. Scholars view the accounts of giants as mythological narratives representing the mixing of divine and human realms, reflecting anxieties about moral corruption and societal disorder during the Second Temple period. The book is considered a product of its time, influenced by Mesopotamian and other ancient Near Eastern mythologies featuring hybrid creatures and divine intervention in human affairs. The accounts of the giants are generally considered symbolic, rather than literal historical accounts.
subtitle: Nephilim: Offspring of Angels and Humans

claims.claim: Giants (Nephilim) were literal offspring of fallen angels and human women.
claims.evidence: Direct textual interpretation of the Book of Enoch and Genesis 6:4.
claims.criticism: Mainstream theology interprets Genesis 6:4 symbolically and views the Book of Enoch as apocryphal, not divinely inspired or historically accurate.

claims.claim: Giant skeletons and archaeological finds prove the existence of Nephilim.
claims.evidence: Reports of large human skeletons discovered throughout history, often lacking verifiable scientific documentation or context. Some sites are misinterpreted.
claims.criticism: Most reports of giant skeletons are unsubstantiated or based on misinterpretations of natural phenomena, such as large animal bones or geological formations. Lack of peer-reviewed scientific evidence.

claims.claim: The Nephilim possessed advanced knowledge or technology.
claims.evidence: Inferences drawn from the Book of Enoch describing the Watchers teaching humans forbidden arts.
claims.criticism: The 'forbidden arts' are interpreted metaphorically by most scholars as representing moral corruption and societal breakdown, not literal technological advancements.

keyFigures.name: James Charlesworth
keyFigures.role: Scholar of the Dead Sea Scrolls and Pseudepigrapha, including the Book of Enoch

keyFigures.name: Cris Putnam
keyFigures.role: Author promoting the literal interpretation of the Book of Enoch and its relation to giants.

keyFigures.name: Michael S. Heiser
keyFigures.role: Biblical scholar who argued for a nuanced understanding of the divine council and its influence on the Enochian texts, but also cautioned against overly literal interpretations.

timeline.event: Earliest fragments of the Book of Enoch appear.
Book of Enoch quoted in the New Testament (Book of Jude).
Interest in giants and the Book of Enoch persists in folklore and theological discussions.
Renewed interest in the Book of Enoch and the Nephilim within alternative history and conspiracy theory circles.

sources.author: Putnam, Cris
sources.work: Petrus Romanus: The Final Pope Is Here
sources.title: Putnam, Cris

sources.author: Heiser, Michael S.
sources.work: The Unseen Realm: Recovering the Supernatural Worldview of the Bible
sources.title: Heiser, Michael S.
